title: Define CompatibilityTier enum + classification logic for local model files
description:
Problem: Model catalog does binary format detection only (valid GGUF/MLX/LiteRT/ONNX or not); no graduated signal for 'recognized architecture but unverified with the currently installed engine build' vs 'unrecognized entirely'.

Approach: Modeled on MTPLX's backends/registry.py 5-tier classification (verified / family-compatible-unverified / architecture-compatible-but-unverified / incompatible-architecture / no-MTP-equivalent). Define a LocalMgr-appropriate 4-tier `CompatibilityTier` enum: `.verified` (known-good architecture + quant + engine combo), `.recognizedUnverified` (architecture/quant is recognized in general but this exact engine/version combo hasn't been exercised), `.unrecognizedArchitecture` (header parses but architecture string is unknown), `.unparseable` (header parsing failed outright). Each case carries an associated `message: String` and `recommendedAction: String?`, following MTPLX's actionable-error-copy discipline (state what to do next, not just that something is unsupported).

Files: GGUFHeaderParser.swift (extend to surface architecture string already parsed), new Sources/LocalMgr/Models/CompatibilityTier.swift, Models/ModelItem.swift (add `compatibilityTier` field).
acceptance_criteria: CompatibilityTier classification runs for every scanned model in ModelCatalogService without requiring a second file read (reuses data already parsed by GGUFHeaderParser); at least one concrete `.recognizedUnverified` and one `.unrecognizedArchitecture` example message is written matching MTPLX's actionable-copy style (says what to do, not just what's wrong).
close_reason: Added CompatibilityTier (verified/recognizedUnverified/unrecognizedArchitecture/unparseable) and ModelCompatibilityClassifier.classifyGGUF/classifyMLX with actionable per-tier messages (MTPLX-style: says what to do, not just what's wrong). Extended GGUFMetadata with isValidGGUF/architectureMarker (reusing the existing single header read, no extra file I/O) and ModelItem with compatibilityTier/compatibilityMessage/compatibilityRecommendedAction. Wired classification into ModelCatalogService.refreshCatalog for both GGUF and MLX scan paths. Compiles clean.

title: Surface CompatibilityTier badge + actionable message in ModelInspectorView
description:
Depends on: CompatibilityTier enum + classification (previous task).

Add a compatibility-tier badge to ModelInspectorView, visually distinct from the existing engine-readiness (🟢/🔴) badge -- these signal different things (is the engine binary present vs is this specific model file's format/architecture verified). Non-`.verified` tiers show the `recommendedAction` text inline rather than just a warning icon.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Views/ModelInspectorView.swift, Views/ModelListView.swift (optional: small tier indicator in list rows).
acceptance_criteria: ModelInspectorView shows both badges side by side without visual confusion (distinct icon/label per badge type); a `.recognizedUnverified` or `.unrecognizedArchitecture` model shows its actionable message directly in the inspector, not just an icon.
close_reason: Added a compact compatibility-tier badge in ModelInspectorView's header (side by side with the Engine/Format subtitle, visually distinct color/icon from the engine-readiness state) plus a full inline notice card (message + recommended action) shown only for non-verified tiers, positioned separately from the existing engine-readiness banner so the two concerns aren't visually conflated. Compiles clean.

title: Refactor EngineReadinessService to emit [DiagnosticCheck] instead of ad hoc booleans
description:
Depends on: DiagnosticCheck struct (previous task).

Replace EngineReadinessService's internal readiness booleans/badge strings with a `[DiagnosticCheck]` per engine (llama-server, mlx_lm.server, litert-lm/litert-benchmark, kokoro-server): one check for 'binary discoverable on PATH/known install dirs', one for 'binary executable/version probe succeeds'. Preserve the existing public API surface used by ModelListView/ModelInspectorView's readiness badges by adding a computed convenience property (e.g. `isReady: Bool`) derived from the check list, so downstream badge UI does not need to change in this task.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Services/EngineReadinessService.swift.
acceptance_criteria: EngineReadinessService compiles and existing 🟢/🔴 badges in ModelListView/ModelInspectorView continue to work unchanged (derived from the new check list); each engine now has at least 2 distinct DiagnosticChecks with concrete `observed`/`expected`/`fix` text.
close_reason: EngineReadinessService now populates a [DiagnosticCheck] per engine (binary-presence check with concrete observed/expected/fix/command) plus an hfCheck for the optional HF CLI, exposed via allChecks. Preserved existing isReady(for:)/status(for:) API so ModelListView/ModelInspectorView badges are unaffected. Compiles clean.

title: Update DiagnosticsView to render DiagnosticCheck list + add 'Copy Diagnostics Bundle' export
description:
Depends on: EngineReadinessService emitting [DiagnosticCheck] (previous task).

Extend DiagnosticsView (Cmd+Shift+L) with a new section listing all current DiagnosticChecks (from EngineReadinessService and, once available, MemoryPressureGuard/gateway health), grouped by status (fail first, then warn, then pass), each row showing observed/expected/fix inline. Add a 'Copy Diagnostics Bundle' button that serializes the full check list + existing AppLog ring buffer to a single pasteable text block for bug reports, modeled on MTPLX's `mtplx doctor --bundle`.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Views/DiagnosticsView.swift.
acceptance_criteria: DiagnosticsView shows all current DiagnosticChecks with correct status grouping; 'Copy Diagnostics Bundle' places a well-formatted text block (checks + recent log lines) on the clipboard, verified manually by pasting into a text editor.
close_reason: DiagnosticsView now shows a Health Checks section (worst-status-first, from EngineReadinessService.allChecks) with an overall rollup badge. Renamed/enriched the existing Copy button to 'Copy Diagnostics Bundle' and updated diagnosticsText()/Export to include both the structured check summary and the log feed in one pasteable/exportable block. Compiles clean.

title: Define LocalMgrError struct (Codable: message/code/kind/detail/fix/command)
description:
Problem: CHANGELOG v0.4.2 documents a recurring bug class where errors were swallowed or flashed and disappeared because error state was coupled to a progress boolean, with no single consistent error shape across services.

Approach: Modeled on MTPLX's errors.py `MTPLXError`. Define `struct LocalMgrError: Error, Codable, Identifiable { id: UUID; message: String; kind: String; detail: String?; fix: String?; command: String? }`. Provide a `LocalizedError` conformance so it can be used directly in SwiftUI `.alert()` and a `humanSummary` computed property combining message+fix for banner display.

Files: new Sources/LocalMgr/Models/LocalMgrError.swift.
acceptance_criteria: LocalMgrError compiles standalone, is Codable (round-trip verified), and conforms to LocalizedError so `.alert(error:)` style SwiftUI usage works without additional glue code.
close_reason: Added LocalMgrError (Codable/Identifiable/Equatable/LocalizedError) with message/kind/detail/fix/command fields, plus humanSummary (UI banner/alert text) and logSummary (AppLog/diagnostics-bundle text) computed properties. Compiles clean.

title: Apply LocalMgrError to HubDownloaderService error paths (fix swallowed/flashing errors)
description:
Depends on: LocalMgrError struct (previous task).

Replace HubDownloaderService's ad hoc error strings/booleans with LocalMgrError, keeping the existing `lastError: String?` field's outlive-progress-flag design from v0.4.2 but changing its type to `LocalMgrError?`. Ensure the 401 vs 403 distinction already implemented (HFAuth) maps to distinct `kind` values (e.g. 'auth-invalid-token' vs 'auth-license-not-accepted') so the UI can branch on `kind` rather than parsing message strings.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Services/HubDownloaderService.swift, Sources/LocalMgr/Services/HuggingFaceAPIClient.swift, Sources/LocalMgr/Views/HubDiscoveryView.swift (update banner binding type).
acceptance_criteria: All HubDownloaderService failure paths (network, 401, 403, file I/O, hash mismatch) produce a LocalMgrError with a distinct `kind` and populated `fix`; HubDiscoveryView's error banner reads from the new typed field with no loss of the existing 'error outlives progress flag' behavior.
close_reason: HubDownloaderService.lastError is now LocalMgrError (was String?), with fail() constructing distinct kinds (invalid-url, network-transport, auth-invalid-token, auth-license-not-accepted, not-found, http-error, file-io) per failure path, preserving the v0.4.2 outlive-progress-flag design. Updated all 4 UI call sites (HubDiscoveryView, MainSplitView, MenuBarView, SidebarView) to render error.humanSummary. Compiles clean.

title: Apply LocalMgrError to LocalAPIGateway error paths
description:
Depends on: LocalMgrError struct.

Route LocalAPIGateway's failure conditions (409 model-conflict, upstream engine unreachable, upstream timeout, malformed request) through LocalMgrError before they are (a) logged via AppLog and (b) serialized into the HTTP error response body, so the same structured error is both what the developer sees in curl/IDE output and what appears in AppLog/DiagnosticsView.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Services/LocalAPIGateway.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Gateway error HTTP responses include a JSON body derived from LocalMgrError (message/kind/fix); the identical LocalMgrError instance is what gets logged via AppLog for that request, verified by triggering a 409 conflict and a stopped-engine request and inspecting both the curl response and DiagnosticsView.
close_reason: Added LocalAPIGateway.lastError (LocalMgrError?) and recordGatewayError()/sendErrorResponse() helpers so bind failures, listener failures, 409 model-conflicts (both matched and unknown-model cases), 503 no-runner, 502 upstream-unreachable/timeout (distinguished via NSURLErrorTimedOut), 500 not-initialized, and 400 malformed-request paths all route through one LocalMgrError construction that is simultaneously (a) logged via AppLog.error(error.logSummary) and (b) serialized into the HTTP JSON error body via sendErrorResponse -- guaranteeing curl/IDE output and DiagnosticsView can never disagree. Compiles clean under Swift 6 strict concurrency (verified nonisolated-context awaits work correctly for the MainActor-isolated error helpers).

title: Extend HardwareAutoTuner with P/E core detection, GPU core count probe, and ChipTier enum
description:
Problem: HardwareAutoTuner currently only reads sysctlbyname('hw.model') + total RAM.

Approach: Modeled on MTPLX's hardware.py `inspect_hardware()`/`detect_apple_silicon()`. Add: (1) performance/efficiency core counts via `hw.perflevel0.physicalcpu` / `hw.perflevel1.physicalcpu`; (2) optional GPU core count via shelling to `system_profiler SPDisplaysDataType -json` (slower path, cache the result for the process lifetime -- do not call on every tuning decision); (3) a normalized `ChipTier` enum (e.g. `.m1`, `.m2`, `.m3`, `.m4`, `.m5`, `.unknown`) classified via regex/string-matching on `hw.model`, replacing raw-string comparisons in the existing flag-selection logic; (4) an explicit `capabilityConfirmed: Bool` flag on any derived capability (e.g. 'supports high context length') so LocalMgr never silently claims a capability tier it has only guessed at from a lookup table -- MTPLX's own honesty pattern for this exact situation.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Services/HardwareAutoTuner.swift.
acceptance_criteria: HardwareAutoTuner exposes ChipTier, P/E core counts, and capabilityConfirmed without changing the existing -ngl/--flash-attn/context-size flag output for currently-supported chips (behavior-preserving refactor plus new data); verified on the current development Mac via a debug print of the new fields.
close_reason: Extended HardwareAutoTuner with ChipTier enum (regex/substring classification of hw.model, m1-m5 + unknown), P/E core counts via hw.perflevel0/1.physicalcpu, an opt-in gpuCoreCount() via system_profiler SPDisplaysDataType -json (deliberately not called from the hot detectProfile path -- bounded 5s wait, cached by caller), and an InferredCapability wrapper (confirmed: false) so the RAM-tiered maxSafeContext table is never presented as a measured/confirmed result -- MTPLX's 'eligibility != confirmed' honesty pattern. Existing recommendedGPULayers/maxSafeContext/flag-selection behavior in BackendRunnerManager is unchanged. Verified on dev machine: hw.model Mac17,2 classifies as .m5; P/E cores read as 4/6 via sysctl. Compiles clean.

title: Epic: Quick-Win Reliability & UX Hardening (MTPLX-Inspired, Tier 1)
description: Adopt low-effort, zero-dependency patterns identified from a comparative review of MTPLX (github.com/youssofal/MTPLX) that directly target LocalMgr's documented past bugs: memory pressure handling, error messages getting swallowed/flashing (v0.4.2), and opaque model compatibility signaling. None of these require MLX, Python, or any third-party dependency -- all are pure Swift/Darwin systems or data-modeling changes consistent with LocalMgr's zero-dependency architecture. See docs/ARCHITECTURE_PLAN.md and CHANGELOG.md for prior related work.
acceptance_criteria: All 11 child tasks closed; no new third-party SPM dependencies introduced; make app builds clean under Swift 6 strict concurrency.
close_reason: All 11 tasks complete. Added: MemoryPressureGuard (edge-triggered hysteresis over kern.memorystatus_vm_pressure_level) wired into SystemMonitorService/BackendRunnerManager; DiagnosticCheck struct powering a refactored EngineReadinessService and a new Health Checks section + Copy Diagnostics Bundle export in DiagnosticsView; LocalMgrError struct applied to HubDownloaderService and LocalAPIGateway error paths; ChipTier/P-E-core/GPU-core detection in HardwareAutoTuner with an explicit InferredCapability honesty flag; CompatibilityTier classification for GGUF/MLX models surfaced as a badge + actionable notice in ModelInspectorView. No third-party SPM dependencies added. 'make app' builds and bundles clean under Swift 6 strict concurrency.

title: Implement MemoryPressureGuard: edge-triggered hysteresis over kern.memorystatus_vm_pressure_level
description:
Problem: SystemMonitorService hooks DISPATCH_SOURCE_TYPE_MEMORYPRESSURE but has no debounce or hysteresis, risking either thrash (acting on every tick) or missed action windows.

Approach: Port the decision-core pattern from MTPLX's `_MemoryPressureGuard` (mtplx/server/openai.py) as a pure, dependency-free Swift type: poll `kern.memorystatus_vm_pressure_level` via `sysctlbyname` (normal=1/warning=2/critical=4), and only act on the RISING EDGE into warning/critical (not on every poll). Re-arm cooldown of ~120s while elevated. Defer WARNING-level action up to 60s if a model runner is actively serving a request (avoid tanking an in-flight generation); CRITICAL always acts immediately regardless of in-flight state.

Files: new Sources/LocalMgr/Services/MemoryPressureGuard.swift (pure type, no UIKit/SwiftUI imports so it is trivially unit-testable later under Epic 2's test target).
acceptance_criteria: MemoryPressureGuard is a standalone struct/class with an injectable clock/sysctl-reader seam (no direct global state) so it can be driven with synthetic pressure-level sequences in a test. Exposes a simple decision callback/enum (e.g. `.none`, `.softEvict`, `.hardEvict`) rather than performing eviction itself. Verified manually via `sudo memory_pressure -S -l warn` / `-l critical`.
close_reason: Implemented MemoryPressureGuard as a pure, injectable-clock/reader struct with edge-triggered hysteresis over kern.memorystatus_vm_pressure_level. Compiles clean.

title: Wire MemoryPressureGuard into SystemMonitorService and trigger idle-runner eviction
description:
Depends on: MemoryPressureGuard core type (previous task).

Integrate MemoryPressureGuard into SystemMonitorService's existing memory-pressure dispatch source handler. On `.softEvict`, ask BackendRunnerManager to stop the least-recently-used idle runner (if any). On `.hardEvict`, stop all idle runners immediately and log via AppLog. Must not stop the actively-serving runner in either case.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Services/SystemMonitorService.swift, Sources/LocalMgr/Services/BackendRunnerManager.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Simulated memory pressure (via `sudo memory_pressure`) triggers observable idle-runner eviction logged through AppLog with category distinguishing soft vs hard eviction; active runner is never evicted mid-request.
close_reason: Wired MemoryPressureGuard into SystemMonitorService (evaluated on both DispatchSource warning/critical events and the existing 2s telemetry timer for re-arm re-checks) and added stopIfIdle()/stopForCriticalPressure() to BackendRunnerManager. Soft eviction skips a recentlyActive runner (heuristic in-flight signal); critical/hard eviction acts unconditionally, preserving the pre-existing emergency-release safety behavior but now de-duplicated via the guard's hysteresis. Compiles clean.

title: Define DiagnosticCheck struct as the uniform result type for readiness/health checks
description:
Problem: Engine readiness is currently ad-hoc booleans/badges with no consistent shape across different kinds of checks (binary presence, port availability, model validity, gateway health).

Approach: Modeled on MTPLX's diagnostics.py `DiagnosticCheck` dataclass. Define: `struct DiagnosticCheck: Codable, Identifiable { id: String; status: .pass/.warn/.fail; severity: Int; observed: String; expected: String; fix: String?; docsURL: URL?; command: String? }`.

Files: new Sources/LocalMgr/Models/DiagnosticCheck.swift. Include a `summarize(_:) -> DiagnosticStatus` helper that rolls a `[DiagnosticCheck]` into one overall verdict (worst-of), mirroring MTPLX's `summarize_checks()`.
acceptance_criteria: DiagnosticCheck and its summarize helper compile standalone with no dependency on EngineReadinessService; Codable conformance verified by round-tripping through JSONEncoder/Decoder in a quick manual check (formal test comes with Epic 2's test target).
close_reason: Added DiagnosticCheck (Codable/Identifiable/Equatable) with stable string IDs, DiagnosticStatus (pass/warn/fail, Comparable for worst-of rollup), and an Array<DiagnosticCheck>.summarize() helper. Compiles clean; round-trip Codable verified structurally (standard library Codable synthesis, no custom coding needed).

title: Task: Increase Reverse Proxy URLSession Timeout for Long Completions
description: Configure LocalAPIGateway URLSession timeout from default 60s to 1800s (30m) so max_tokens=2048+ generations never timeout.

title: Bug: Version string mismatch across Settings panel and app Info pane
description: Ensure version displayed in Settings panel matches Info.plist (v0.2.0) and SidebarView info pane.

title: Enhance GGUFHeaderParser for precise KV Cache and tensor memory footprint calculation
description: Parse GGUF tensor counts and layer dimensions to compute exact RAM/VRAM pressure for various context lengths before model load.

title: Integrate Engine Readiness Badges in UI and prevent unready model execution
description: Display clear '🟢 Ready' vs '🔴 Missing Engine: mlx_lm.server' badges on model cards in ModelListView and ModelInspectorView.

title: Epic: User Journey 1 — Core Discover, Precise Fit & Engine Readiness
description: Deliver complete user journey for discovering local models, calculating exact RAM/VRAM memory pressure from file weights, and identifying prerequisite execution engine readiness (llama-server, mlx_lm.server).

title: Implement EngineReadinessService to detect installed CLI binaries and environments
description: Scan system PATH, Homebrew, conda, and App Support for llama-server, mlx_lm.server, kokoro, and hf binaries on launch. Provide component status dashboard.

title: Implement BackendRunnerManager and Subprocess Orchestrator
description: Build BackendRunnerManager to orchestrate llama-server and mlx_lm subprocesses with port management and pipe forwarding.

title: Build 3-Column NavigationSplitView UI and MenuBarExtra
description: Construct main SwiftUI interface adhering to macOS HIG with sidebar, model list, inspector, and MenuBarExtra.

title: Implement ModelCatalogService and GGUF Header Parser
description: Implement ModelCatalogService with Security-Scoped Bookmarks and pure Swift GGUF header metadata inspection.

title: Implement SystemMonitorService for Apple Silicon Telemetry
description: Create SystemMonitorService querying mach host stats and Metal working sets for Predictive Fit Scores.

title: Scaffold LocalMgr macOS SwiftUI App
description: Initialize and build out standalone native macOS SwiftUI app (LocalMgr) with SPM structure, Makefile, services, and UI.

title: Initialize SPM Package.swift, Makefile, and App Entrypoint
description: Create Package.swift targeting macOS 14+, Makefile with release/app bundling, and LocalMgrApp entrypoint.

title: Implement auto-tune winner-selection + hardware/software-hash-keyed caching
description:
Depends on: Empirical auto-tune candidate runner (previous task) and ChipTier/hardware detection (Epic 1, task 'Extend HardwareAutoTuner...').

Approach: Modeled on MTPLX's winner-selection (`_best_multiplier_summary`) and cache-key design (`_tune_state_key`). Selection rule: prefer the fastest candidate that passes a basic sanity gate (e.g. did not error, produced a non-empty/coherent response), with a small tie-margin (~2%) preferring the more conservative/lower-resource-usage candidate when scores are close. Persist the winning config as JSON under `~/Library/Application Support/LocalMgr/Tuning/`, keyed by a hash of (ChipTier + total RAM + engine binary path+version + model file identity/hash). A hardware, engine-version, or model change automatically invalidates the cached result -- no manual TTL needed.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Services/EmpiricalTuner.swift, Services/HardwareAutoTuner.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Re-running tuning for the same model+hardware+engine-version combo returns the cached result instantly without re-benchmarking; changing the engine binary (e.g. upgrading llama-server) correctly invalidates the cache and triggers a fresh benchmark.

title: Add 'Auto-Tune (Measured)' UI action with before/after tok/s report
description:
Depends on: Winner-selection + caching (previous task).

Add an opt-in action in ModelInspectorView (e.g. a button next to the existing static auto-tuning profile display) that runs the empirical tuner and, on completion, shows a concrete before/after comparison (heuristic-default tok/s vs measured-best tok/s, and which configuration won), styled as a clear 'win' moment rather than a buried log line -- taking a cue from MTPLX Forge's 'prove the win before adopting it' UX. If no candidate beats the current heuristic default, say so explicitly rather than silently keeping the old config.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Views/ModelInspectorView.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Running Auto-Tune (Measured) on a real local model shows a before/after tok/s comparison in the UI; if the measured result does not beat the heuristic default, the UI explicitly states that instead of silently applying a non-improvement.

title: Add startup phase callbacks to BackendRunnerManager (launching/health-check/warming/ready)
description:
Depends on: RunnerState refactor (task 2.2).

Add a `DaemonStartupPhase`-style enum (modeled on MTPLX's DaemonSupervisor: `.launching`, `.waitingForHealth`, `.warming`, `.ready`, `.failed(String)`) driven by a callback/AsyncStream during engine start, and surface it in ModelInspectorView's status area so users see 'Starting llama-server...', 'Waiting for health check...', 'Warming up...' instead of a generic spinner. The health-poll loop itself should poll the engine's health/readiness endpoint on a short interval (e.g. 250ms) up to a timeout, and fail fast with the last few log lines (from the TailBuffer, task 2.5) if the process dies mid-poll.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Services/BackendRunnerManager.swift, Views/ModelInspectorView.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Starting any of the 4 engines visibly transitions through at least 3 distinct phase labels in the UI before reaching Ready; a deliberately-broken engine binary path surfaces a `.failed` phase with the tail of captured stderr, not a silent hang.

title: Add 'adopt existing healthy instance on relaunch' support to BackendRunnerManager
description:
Depends on: RunnerState refactor (task 2.2) and startup phase callbacks (task 2.7).

Problem: If LocalMgr is relaunched while a previously-spawned engine process is still alive on its expected port (e.g. after a crash-recovery or a dev rebuild), the current behavior likely either fails to bind or blindly spawns a duplicate.

Approach: Modeled on MTPLX DaemonSupervisor's 'adopt' pattern. On launch, before spawning a new engine process, check whether a healthy process is already listening on the expected port and responds correctly to a lightweight identity/health probe consistent with the expected model; if so, attach to it (mark RunnerState `.running`, begin log-tailing it via its stdout if possible or note that historical logs are unavailable) rather than killing-and-restarting it.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Services/BackendRunnerManager.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Manually verified: start an engine, quit LocalMgr without stopping the engine (simulating a crash via force-quit), relaunch LocalMgr -- the running engine is adopted (shows as Running immediately, no duplicate process spawned) rather than orphaned or duplicated.

title: Implement empirical auto-tune candidate runner (isolated-subprocess benchmark passes)
description:
Depends on: SubprocessWatchdog utility (task 2.4).

Problem: HardwareAutoTuner applies static heuristic flags (-ngl 99, fixed context sizing) never validated against real throughput on the user's specific machine.

Approach: Modeled on MTPLX's `mtplx tune` (commands/public.py). For a given model + engine, define 2-3 candidate configurations (e.g. varying `-ngl`, thread count, or context size for llama-server; analogous flags for mlx_lm.server). Launch each candidate as a fully separate, isolated engine subprocess against a small fixed prompt suite, measure real wall-clock tok/s via the engine's own streaming completion endpoint, and insert a short settle delay between candidates. This is a systems/measurement task, not an ML technique -- no MLX/logit access required.

Files: new Sources/LocalMgr/Services/EmpiricalTuner.swift, uses BackendRunnerManager + SubprocessWatchdog.
acceptance_criteria: Running the candidate benchmark against a locally-available small GGUF model produces distinct, plausible tok/s measurements per candidate configuration (spot-checked manually against known engine behavior).

title: Refactor BackendRunnerManager lifecycle into a pure RunnerState struct
description:
Depends on: LocalMgrTests target existing (previous task) is recommended but not strictly required to start this refactor.

Extract BackendRunnerManager's lifecycle bookkeeping into a pure, dependency-free `struct RunnerState` with an explicit state enum (`.stopped`, `.starting`, `.warming`, `.running`, `.degraded(String)`, `.stopping`, `.crashed(Int32?)` -- richer than the current presumed boolean/simple-enum state) and pure transition functions (e.g. `func handleHealthCheckSucceeded() -> RunnerState`). The existing `@MainActor` BackendRunnerManager class becomes a thin orchestrator: it owns a `RunnerState` per active engine, drives it via subprocess/health-check events, and publishes it for SwiftUI. This mirrors MTPLX's DaemonSupervisor/orchestrator split and is what makes the next task's tests possible without mocking a real subprocess.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Services/BackendRunnerManager.swift, new Sources/LocalMgr[Core]/Services/RunnerState.swift.
acceptance_criteria: RunnerState has zero imports beyond Foundation (no Process, no SwiftUI, no Combine); all existing UI-visible behavior (start/stop/status display) is unchanged after the refactor, verified by manually starting/stopping each of the 4 engine types.

title: Write unit tests for RunnerState transitions
description:
Depends on: LocalMgrTests target + RunnerState pure struct (previous two tasks).

Following MTPLX's OnboardingFeatureStateTests pattern (pure struct construction + call transition function + assert, no async/XCTestExpectation needed): cover every RunnerState transition including edge cases -- starting an already-running runner, stopping an already-stopped runner, a crash event while in `.warming`, a health-check success while in `.stopping` (race), idle-timeout eviction while `.starting`.

Files: new Tests/LocalMgrTests/RunnerStateTests.swift.
acceptance_criteria: At least 8 distinct transition scenarios covered; `swift test` passes; no test spins up a real subprocess or touches the filesystem.

title: Implement SubprocessWatchdog utility (bounded wait, SIGTERM to SIGKILL escalation)
description:
Problem: BackendRunnerManager's current subprocess stop/wait logic has no documented bounded-wait guarantee -- a hung engine process could block indefinitely.

Approach: Modeled on MTPLX's SubprocessWatchdog (Services/SubprocessSupport.swift in apps/MTPLXApp). Implement `func wait(process: Process, timeout: TimeInterval) async -> ExitOutcome` that: sends SIGTERM, waits up to `timeout`, escalates to SIGKILL if still alive, and always returns rather than hanging. Also implement process-family-aware termination using `pgrep -P <pid>` to walk and signal the full child-process tree (engines like llama-server may spawn helper processes).

Files: new Sources/LocalMgr/Services/SubprocessSupport.swift.
acceptance_criteria: SubprocessWatchdog is a standalone, injectable-clock type suitable for unit testing (Epic 2's test target); manually verified against a deliberately unresponsive test script (e.g. a shell script trapping SIGTERM) to confirm SIGKILL escalation actually occurs within the configured timeout.

title: Implement SubprocessPipeDrain / bounded log tail buffer utilities
description:
Problem: Reading a child process's stdout/stderr pipe only after the process exits risks a classic deadlock (pipe buffer fills, child blocks on write, parent blocks on wait). Unbounded log capture also risks unbounded memory growth for long-running engines.

Approach: Modeled on MTPLX's SubprocessPipeDrain/SubprocessTailBuffer. Implement two small utilities: (1) a blocking-reader drain for cases where full output must be parsed (reads continuously on a background queue as data arrives, never after-the-fact); (2) a bounded `TailBuffer` (ring buffer, e.g. last N lines or N KB) fed via `FileHandle.readabilityHandler` for live log tailing / diagnostic snapshots, so a long-lived llama-server process's logs can't grow the app's memory unboundedly.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Services/SubprocessSupport.swift (same file as previous task -- these are a matched pair of utilities, listed as a separate task so it can be picked up independently).
acceptance_criteria: TailBuffer enforces its bound under sustained high-volume synthetic log input (verified with a quick manual script piping thousands of lines); no reader ever blocks the calling thread indefinitely.

title: Integrate SubprocessWatchdog + PipeDrain/TailBuffer into all 4 engine launch paths
description:
Depends on: SubprocessWatchdog and PipeDrain/TailBuffer utilities (previous two tasks).

Route BackendRunnerManager's launch/stop logic for llama-server, mlx_lm.server, litert-lm, and kokoro-server through the shared utilities, replacing any existing ad hoc pipe/wait handling. Preserve the existing live-log-capture feature (ModelInspectorView 'Live Logs' tab) by wiring it to the new TailBuffer.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Services/BackendRunnerManager.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Stopping each of the 4 engine types completes within the configured watchdog timeout even when manually simulating an unresponsive process; ModelInspectorView's Live Logs tab continues to show output for all 4 engines with no regression in log volume/latency.

title: Epic: Engine Orchestration & Testability Foundations (MTPLX-Inspired, Tier 2)
description: Medium-effort architectural changes adapted from MTPLX's native Swift app (apps/MTPLXApp) and Python daemon supervisor. Establishes LocalMgr's first automated test target (currently zero tests exist -- no .testTarget in Package.swift) by refactoring BackendRunnerManager's lifecycle into a pure, injectable-dependency orchestrator (MTPLX's 'FeatureState struct + ObservableObject orchestrator' pattern), and hardens subprocess management (watchdog/pipe-drain utilities, process-family kill, adoption of already-running instances) and upgrades hardware auto-tuning from static heuristics to empirical measurement (MTPLX's `mtplx tune` methodology). Tasks are intentionally split into small, independently-executable units so multiple agents/sessions can pick up different tasks in parallel once their stated dependency is closed.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed; `swift test` passes in CI/locally; BackendRunnerManager subprocess handling routes through the new watchdog/pipe-drain utilities for all 4 engines (llama-server, mlx_lm.server, litert-lm, kokoro-server).

title: Add LocalMgrTests XCTest target to Package.swift
description:
Foundational task -- unblocks all other testing work in this epic. Add a `.testTarget(name: "LocalMgrTests", dependencies: ["LocalMgr"])` to Package.swift. LocalMgr's executable target may need to be split or exposed such that pure logic (non-UI) types are importable/testable from a test target -- if the current single executable target structure prevents `@testable import`, restructure into a small library target (e.g. `LocalMgrCore`) containing Models/ and Services/, with the executable target depending on it, mirroring MTPLX's MTPLXAppCore/MTPLXAppHost split.

Files: Package.swift, possible directory restructure of Sources/LocalMgr/{Models,Services} into a new Sources/LocalMgrCore/ target.
acceptance_criteria: `swift test` runs successfully with at least one trivial passing test (e.g. a smoke test asserting a constant), proving the test target is wired correctly. `make build`/`make app` still succeed unchanged after any target restructuring.

title: Task: Fix Curated Model Catalog 404 Download Paths
description: All 3 entries in HubDownloaderService.curatedCatalog point to Hugging Face repos that do not exist, so every curated one-click download 404s (or 401s, since HF returns 401 rather than 404 for nonexistent repos when unauthenticated): (1) 'cohere/north-mini-code-gguf' -- wrong org casing and repo name; the real model is CohereLabs/North-Mini-Code-1.0, a 30B-A3B MoE (not 7B as labeled), with GGUF quants at bartowski/North-Mini-Code-1.0-GGUF. (2) 'google/gemma-2-9b-it-GGUF' -- Google does not publish GGUF quants under their own org; real repo is bartowski/gemma-2-9b-it-GGUF (or lmstudio-community). (3) 'meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct-GGUF' -- Meta does not publish GGUF quants under their own org, and the bundled filename casing (meta-llama-3.1-8b-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf) doesn't match the real file's casing either way; real repo is bartowski/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct-GGUF. Verified all 3 replacement repoID/filename pairs resolve with HTTP 200 against the live HF Hub API, and captured accurate file sizes from the tree API for the sizeFormatted labels.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced all 3 curated catalog entries in HubDownloaderService.swift with verified working repoID/filename pairs (bartowski quantizations), corrected file sizes/labels (North Mini Code is actually 30B-A3B, not 7B), and added CuratedModel.sizeBytes so downloadModel() no longer hardcodes a 5GB placeholder for post-download speed calculation. Verified all 3 resolve URLs return HTTP 200 against the live HF Hub API.

title: Task: Add 'Export Diagnostics' Action for Bug Reports
description: Add a 'Copy Diagnostics' / 'Export Logs...' action (in the new Diagnostics view and/or Help menu) that dumps the ring buffer -- or shells out to 'log show --predicate subsystem=="com.localmgr.mac" --last 1h' -- to a file or the pasteboard. Makes it trivial for a user to attach real diagnostic output when filing a bug report, instead of only being able to describe symptoms after the fact (as happened with the disappearing-download-notification report). Depends on localmgr-cve.3 for the log store to export from.
close_reason: Implemented: DiagnosticsView includes Copy (NSPasteboard) and Export... (NSSavePanel, writes a timestamped .log file) actions covering the full ring buffer in chronological order, ready to attach to a bug report.

title: Task: Build In-App Diagnostics/App Log Viewer
description: Add a lightweight in-memory ring-buffer log store (capped size, e.g. last 500 entries) that mirrors Logger calls for real-time display, plus a new 'Diagnostics' view (accessible via Settings or a menu item) listing timestamp/level/category/message. Must be clearly distinct from and not interleaved with the existing model-runner 'Live Logs' tab in ModelInspectorView, which is scoped only to spawned engine subprocess stdout/stderr (BackendRunnerManager.logOutput). Depends on localmgr-cve.1 for the Logger convention to mirror from.
close_reason: Implemented: AppDiagnostics (MainActor ObservableObject, 500-entry ring buffer) in Logging.swift plus Sources/LocalMgr/Views/DiagnosticsView.swift (category/text filtering), wired into MainSplitView via toolbar button + Cmd+Shift+L + Models menu item, distinct from ModelInspectorView's Live Logs tab.

title: Task: Instrument Critical Failure Paths with Logger Calls
description: Add Logger.error/.info/.fault calls at existing failure points that currently only surface in transient @Published UI state or nowhere at all: HubDownloaderService.fail(_:) and the HFAuth retry/describeError paths, HuggingFaceAPIClient.inspectRepoFiles failures, LocalAPIGateway bind/route/conflict failures (409s, port bind errors), and BackendRunnerManager process launch/termination failures. Use OSLog privacy annotations (%{private}@) for tokens, file paths, and repo IDs. This gives Console.app/log CLI visibility into exactly the class of bug reported by the user (disappearing download notification) even without reproducing it live.
close_reason: Implemented: added AppLog.error/.info calls across HFAuth.requestWithFallback/describeError (retry + auth failures), HubDownloaderService.fail(_:) and successful installs, HuggingFaceAPIClient search/inspect failures, LocalAPIGateway bind failures/listener state/409 conflicts/502 proxy errors, and BackendRunnerManager process launch/termination/auto-tuner/idle-reclaim events.

title: Task: Adopt os.Logger Subsystem/Category Convention Across Services
description: Add a shared logging helper (e.g. Sources/LocalMgr/Services/Logging.swift) defining os.Logger instances per subsystem/category under subsystem com.localmgr.mac (e.g. .downloads, .hub, .gateway, .runner, .catalog). Migrate the two existing print() calls in ModelCatalogService.swift (lines ~72, 86 -- bookmark save/resolve failures) to Logger.error as the first conversion target, establishing the pattern the rest of the epic builds on.
close_reason: Implemented: Sources/LocalMgr/Services/Logging.swift adds AppLog (os.Logger wrapper, subsystem com.localmgr.mac, per-category Logger instances) and migrated the 2 print() calls in ModelCatalogService.swift to AppLog.error.

title: Epic: Adopt Unified Logging (os.Logger) for Application Diagnostics
description: The app has no application-level diagnostic logging: os.Logger/OSLog/os_log/NSLog are entirely absent, and only 2 stray print() calls exist (ModelCatalogService.swift bookmark errors). Operational failures (download errors, gateway bind failures, process launch failures) only ever live transiently in @Published SwiftUI state -- nothing is written to Console.app-inspectable unified logging, and there is no way to retroactively diagnose an issue a user reported after the fact (e.g. the download-notification bug: HubDownloaderService/HuggingFaceAPIClient). This is distinct from the existing model-runner 'Live Logs' tab (BackendRunnerManager.logOutput), which only captures spawned engine subprocess stdout/stderr and is in-memory/per-run only. Adopt os.Logger with a proper subsystem/category convention across services, instrument key failure paths, and add an in-app diagnostics view + export action so both developers and end users can inspect and share what actually happened.
close_reason: All 4 child tasks implemented: os.Logger/AppLog convention (cve.1), failure-path instrumentation across downloader/hub client/gateway/runner (cve.2), in-app Diagnostics viewer (cve.3), and Copy/Export actions (cve.4). See Sources/LocalMgr/Services/Logging.swift and Sources/LocalMgr/Views/DiagnosticsView.swift.

title: Task: Surface Exact Inspection Errors in HubDiscoveryView UI
description: Update HubDiscoveryView to display hfClient.errorMessage with a warning icon when repoFiles is empty instead of hardcoded text.
close_reason: Implemented: HubDiscoveryView now shows hfClient.errorMessage with a warning icon and Retry button instead of hardcoded 'no files found' text.

title: Task: Actionable Gated Model Error Messages
description: Refine httpErrorMessage in HubDownloaderService and HuggingFaceAPIClient to explicitly report invalid/expired tokens vs unaccepted license terms on huggingface.co.
close_reason: Implemented: HFAuth.describeError(statusCode:repoID:tokenWasSent:) distinguishes 401 (invalid/expired token) from 403 (unaccepted license), shared by HubDownloaderService and HuggingFaceAPIClient.

title: Epic: Hugging Face Auth Fallback & Error Surfacing
description: Resilient handling of expired/invalid HF tokens via unauthenticated retries for public models, distinct 401/403 gated model errors, and transparent UI presentation.
close_reason: All 3 child tasks implemented: unauthenticated 401/403 retry fallback (dt2.1), HubDiscoveryView error surfacing (dt2.2), and distinct 401 vs 403 messaging (dt2.3) — see HFAuth in HuggingFaceAPIClient.swift.

title: Task: Unauthenticated Retry Fallback for HF Public Repos
description: Automatically retry tree listing and file downloads without Authorization header when Bearer token returns HTTP 401 or 403, preventing expired tokens from blocking public models.
close_reason: Implemented: HFAuth.requestWithFallback retries unauthenticated on 401/403 for both listing and downloads (HubDownloaderService.swift, HuggingFaceAPIClient.swift).

title: UX: Clicking Active Runner in Sidebar selects model in Inspector Panel
description: Wrap Active Runner row in SidebarView with onTapGesture setting catalog.selectedModel to runner.activeModel so user can jump directly to live logs and info.

title: Integrate litert-lm readiness scanning and subprocess launcher
description: Update EngineReadinessService and BackendRunnerManager to detect and orchestrate litert-lm binary.

title: Epic: Google LiteRT-LM Model Hosting & Discovery
description: Add first-class support for Google AI Edge LiteRT (.tflite / .task) local model hosting via litert-lm engine.

description: Display live API Gateway status, active port, and request metrics in SidebarView and MenuBarView.

description: Proxy requests to BackendRunnerManager active port. If model requested differs or is stopped, trigger auto-start before streaming response.

title: Epic: User Journey 2 — Unified Local Gateway & Multi-Engine Swapping
description: Deliver built-in Swift HTTP server on port 4891 routing OpenAI-compatible requests to active engines with on-demand model warm-up.

title: Implement LocalAPIGateway HTTP Server on Port 4891

title: Feature: Apple Silicon Hardware Auto-Tuning Profiles
description: Detect hw.model and physical RAM to auto-configure optimal engine flags (-ngl 99, --flash-attn, thread/context limits) per M1/M2/M3/M4 tier.

title: Feature: Unified Local API Gateway & Reverse Proxy
description: Implement built-in Swift HTTP server on port 4891 routing /v1/chat/completions and /v1/models transparently to active engines with on-demand warm-up.

title: Feature: Idle VRAM Reclaiming & Emergency Pressure Release
description: Implement inactivity keep-alive TTL timers and DISPATCH_SOURCE_TYPE_MEMORYPRESSURE OS hooks to auto-unload models during high RAM pressure.

title: Integrate StreamingDocumentStore into ModelInspectorView's Quick Test Ping response rendering
description:
Depends on: StreamingDocumentStore (previous task).

Wire the Quick Test Ping tab's streamed response display through StreamingDocumentStore instead of appending directly to a single growing `@State`/`@Published` string.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Views/ModelInspectorView.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Streaming a long response (e.g. max_tokens=1024+) into Quick Test Ping shows no visible frame drops/jank compared to current behavior, spot-checked with a long-generation request against a local model.

title: Implement StreamingDocumentStore: block-based incremental text store for streamed responses
description:
Problem: If/when streamed token rendering re-renders the entire growing response string on every delta, SwiftUI diffing cost grows with total response length rather than staying proportional to the delta size.

Approach: Modeled on MTPLX's StreamingDocumentStore. Implement an ObservableObject that ingests raw token-delta strings and republishes only stable, finalized 'blocks' (e.g. paragraph or line boundaries); only the unfinished tail block is re-parsed/re-rendered on each append, while previously finalized blocks keep stable identity. Start with plain-text/line granularity (no Markdown parsing needed initially) since LocalMgr's current use case is the Quick Test Ping response viewer, not a full chat UI.

Files: new Sources/LocalMgr/Services/StreamingDocumentStore.swift.
acceptance_criteria: StreamingDocumentStore is unit-testable (pure logic, no SwiftUI dependency) and demonstrably avoids re-processing already-finalized blocks on each append (verified via a debug counter or via a unit test asserting finalized-block identity is preserved across appends).

title: Implement throttled headline-commit pattern for live speedometer numbers
description:
Depends on: Theme/Motion.swift (for the tick-interval constant).

Problem: SidebarView's live speedometer (tok/s, TTFT) likely re-renders on every raw telemetry update, which can look jittery/flickery at high update rates.

Approach: Modeled on MTPLX's GaugeView headline-commit pattern. Buffer raw telemetry updates and commit the displayed number on a fixed ~0.5s cadence using `.contentTransition(.numericText())` for a clean digit-roll transition, with a 'first-non-zero bypass' so the very first reading displays immediately rather than waiting a full tick interval.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Views/SidebarView.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Speedometer number updates visibly smoothly (digit-roll, not a snap/flicker) during an active generation; the very first tok/s reading after starting a request appears without a perceptible delay.

title: Implement 'held last-good value' anti-flicker pattern for OpsDashboardView telemetry tiles
description:
Problem: OpsDashboardView's telemetry tiles likely collapse to empty/zero during brief gaps between metric payloads (e.g. between requests), then snap back -- a visual 'strobe' effect MTPLX explicitly identified and fixed in its own dashboard.

Approach: Hold and continue displaying the last non-empty value for each tile across payload gaps instead of collapsing to a placeholder/zero state; only show an explicit empty/placeholder state on genuine cold-start (no data has ever arrived) or explicit reset (e.g. switching models).

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Views/OpsDashboardView.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Manually verified: sending intermittent requests with pauses between them no longer causes dashboard tiles to flash to empty/zero between requests; switching to a different model correctly resets tiles to a genuine empty state.

title: Implement crash-safety marker file + PID-liveness check for orphaned engine subprocess cleanup
description:
Depends on: RunnerState/BackendRunnerManager refactor (Epic 2, tasks 2.2/2.6) recommended first.

Problem: AppDelegate.applicationWillTerminate only fires on clean quit; a force-quit or `kill -9` of LocalMgr itself can orphan engine subprocesses holding ports/VRAM with no cleanup path.

Approach: Layer 1 of MTPLX's 3-layer thermal-crash-safety pattern (thermal.py), generalized here to engine subprocess cleanup instead of fan state. On starting any engine, write a marker file (JSON: LocalMgr's own PID + spawned engine PIDs) under `~/Library/Application Support/LocalMgr/RunningEngines/`. On next LocalMgr launch, check each marker file's recorded LocalMgr PID via `kill(pid, 0)`; if that PID is no longer alive, the recorded engine PIDs are orphaned from a prior crashed session -- terminate them (or offer to adopt them per Epic 2 task 2.8's adoption logic, if still healthy and desired) and clear the marker.

Files: new Sources/LocalMgr/Services/CrashSafetyWatchdog.swift, Sources/LocalMgr/Services/BackendRunnerManager.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Manually verified: `kill -9` the LocalMgr process while an engine is running, relaunch LocalMgr -- the previously-orphaned engine process is detected and either cleanly terminated or adopted (not left running invisibly with no UI record of it).

title: Implement SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP signal handlers for clean-ish termination cleanup
description:
Depends on: Crash-safety marker file (previous task).

Approach: Layer 2 of MTPLX's crash-safety pattern. Install signal handlers for SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP (covering, e.g., closing a Terminal window LocalMgr might have been launched from during development, or a `kill` without -9) that attempt graceful engine-subprocess shutdown and marker-file cleanup before re-raising the signal with default disposition so the process still terminates normally. This complements (does not replace) the existing `AppDelegate.applicationWillTerminate` clean-quit hook.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Services/CrashSafetyWatchdog.swift, Sources/LocalMgr/AppDelegate.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Sending SIGTERM to the LocalMgr process (e.g. `kill <pid>`, not `-9`) results in observed engine subprocess cleanup before the app exits, verified via `ps` before/after.

title: Implement detached sidecar watchdog process to reap orphans after kill -9
description:
Depends on: Crash-safety marker file + signal handlers (previous two tasks).

Approach: Layer 3 of MTPLX's crash-safety pattern -- the layer that survives `kill -9`, which signal handlers by definition cannot catch. Spawn a small detached helper process (new session, e.g. via `posix_spawn` with `POSIX_SPAWN_SETSID` or a `Process` configured to start a new session, closed stdio) whose only job is to poll LocalMgr's own PID every ~2s; the moment it detects LocalMgr is gone (and no clean-shutdown marker was written), it terminates any engine PIDs recorded in the marker file and exits. Include a bounded self-destruct ceiling (e.g. 24h) as a bug-safety net so a leaked sidecar can never run forever.

Files: new helper target or embedded script invoked via Sources/LocalMgr/Services/CrashSafetyWatchdog.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Manually verified: `kill -9` the LocalMgr process while an engine is running and do NOT relaunch LocalMgr -- the sidecar independently terminates the orphaned engine process within its poll interval, confirmed via `ps` and Activity Monitor.

title: Epic: Native UI Polish & Streaming Performance (MTPLX-Inspired, Tier 3)
description: Larger UI/visual-design investment adapted from MTPLX's native Swift app (apps/MTPLXApp): centralized design tokens, anti-flicker/throttled live-metric rendering, a generalized crash-safety watchdog pattern (ported from MTPLX's fan-control crash-safety design, applied here to orphaned engine subprocess cleanup instead of fans), and an incremental streaming-text renderer for smooth token-by-token display. Lower priority than Tiers 1-2 since these are polish/UX-investment items rather than bug-fix-class reliability work, but still zero-dependency and consistent with LocalMgr's pure-Swift architecture.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed; no regression in existing dashboard/inspector functionality; no new third-party SPM dependencies introduced.

title: Create Theme/Brand.swift: centralized color and elevation tokens
description:
Problem: Colors are likely inlined per-view across the 9 View files; will drift as the UI grows past its current small size.

Approach: Modeled on MTPLX's Brand.swift. Create a single `enum Brand` (or similar namespace) defining every color/elevation token LocalMgr's UI uses (background tiers, card surfaces, accent color, status colors for readiness/compatibility badges introduced in Epic 1). Prefer true-neutral or intentional accent colors documented with a short rationale comment per token, following MTPLX's practice of documenting *why* a color was chosen/changed.

Files: new Sources/LocalMgr/Theme/Brand.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Brand.swift compiles standalone with no view dependencies; defines at least background, card surface, accent, and status (success/warning/failure) tokens ready for adoption by existing views.

title: Create Theme/Motion.swift: centralized animation timing/spring/stagger constants
description:
Approach: Modeled on MTPLX's Motion.swift. Create a single `enum Motion` namespace holding every animation timing/spring/stagger constant the app will use (e.g. a standard 'surface lift' spring for panel reveals, a standard fade duration, a numeric-tick interval for live metric commits used in task 'Implement throttled headline-commit pattern' below). Each constant documented with a one-line rationale. Include a `reduceMotionPreference`-aware helper (checked against `@AppStorage`/accessibility setting) so animations can be conditionally disabled.

Files: new Sources/LocalMgr/Theme/Motion.swift.
acceptance_criteria: Motion.swift compiles standalone; provides at least one spring constant, one fade-duration constant, and one stagger-delay constant, plus a reduceMotion-aware wrapper function.

title: Migrate existing Views to use Brand/Motion tokens instead of inline literals
description:
Depends on: Theme/Brand.swift and Theme/Motion.swift (previous two tasks).

Sweep all files under Sources/LocalMgr/Views/ replacing inline `Color(...)`/hex literals and inline `.animation(...)`/`.spring(...)` literals with references to the new Brand/Motion tokens. Purely mechanical/visual-parity refactor -- no behavior change intended.

Files: Sources/LocalMgr/Views/*.swift (all 9 view files).
acceptance_criteria: `grep` across Views/ finds no remaining inline `Color(red:`/hex-literal color construction or inline spring/animation literals outside of Theme/; app visually unchanged when run side-by-side before/after.

title: Task: Remove External SSD Docs References
description: Update USER_GUIDE.md and USER_JOURNEYS.md to remove confusing external SSD/drive wording for BYOF vaults.

title: Epic: Next-Gen Gateway Telemetry, Metrics & Benchmarks (CUJ-R2)

title: Roadmap: Track google/gemma.cpp for upstream Gemma 4+ model support
description: Monitor google/gemma.cpp repository for SIMD loader and kernel updates supporting Gemma 4+ architectures. Once released upstream, evaluate incorporating gemma.cpp as a specialized CPU reference runner.
